RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.amazons3.CompleteBlobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Complete a blob upload that was written directly to cache, but has not yet been completed or aborted.This is an internal only request.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Must be HTTP request type POST
Must include an S3 bucket specification
Must include an S3 object specification
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/{bucket}/{object}?blob={unique identifier or attribute}&job={unique identifier or attribute}[&size={64-bit integer}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.amazons3.CreateBucketRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Create a bucket. This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTBucketPUT.html for AWS documentation).
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Cannot include an S3 object specification
Must be HTTP request type PUT
Must include an S3 bucket specification

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.amazons3.CreateObjectRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Create an S3 object in a bucket. Include the data for the object with your request. It is recommended to use DS3 requests to create a PUT job before using a PUT request to put an object in the job rather than using this request stand alone in a strict S3 manner. This is particularly important when putting many small objects or putting very large objects that must be broken up for physical placement. This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTObjectPUT.html for AWS documentation).
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Must be HTTP request type PUT
Must include an S3 bucket specification
Must include an S3 object specification

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.amazons3.DeleteBucketRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delete a bucket. This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTBucketDELETE.html for AWS documentation).
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Cannot include an S3 object specification
Must be HTTP request type DELETE
Must include an S3 bucket specification

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.amazons3.DeleteObjectRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delete an S3 object in a bucket. This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTObjectDELETE.html for AWS documentation).
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Must be HTTP request type DELETE
Must include an S3 bucket specification
Must include an S3 object specification

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.amazons3.DeleteObjectsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delete multiple S3 objects in a bucket. This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/multiobjectdeleteapi.html for AWS documentation).
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Cannot include an S3 object specification
Must be HTTP request type POST
Must include an S3 bucket specification

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.amazons3.GetBucketRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get S3 objects in a bucket. This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTBucketGET.html for AWS documentation).
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Cannot include an S3 object specification
Must be HTTP request type GET
Must include an S3 bucket specification
Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [delimiter, marker, maxKeys, prefix, versions]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.amazons3.GetBucketsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get all buckets. This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTServiceGET.html for AWS documentation).
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Cannot include an S3 bucket specification
Cannot include an S3 object specification
Must be HTTP request type GET

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.amazons3.GetObjectRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get the data for an S3 object in a bucket. It is recommended to use DS3 requests to create a GET job before using a GET request to get an object in the job rather than using this request stand alone in a strict S3 manner. This is particularly important when getting many small objects or getting very large objects that have been broken up for physical placement. This is an AWS request (see http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/RESTObjectGET.html for AWS documentation).
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Must be HTTP request type GET
Must include an S3 bucket specification
Must include an S3 object specification
Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [cached_only, job, offset, version_id]
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/{bucket}/{object}[?cached_only][&job={unique identifier or attribute}][&offset={64-bit integer}][&version_id={unique identifier or attribute}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.amazons3.HeadBucketRequestHandler

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.amazons3.HeadObjectRequestHandler

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.cache.ForceFullCacheReclaimRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Forces a full reclaim of all caches, and waits until the reclaim completes. Cache contents that need to be retained since they're part of an active job will be retained. Any cache contents that can be reclaimed will be. This operation may take a very long time to complete, depending on how much of the cache can be reclaimed and how many blobs the cache is managing.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.dataplanner.GetDataPlannerBlobStoreTasksRequestHandler

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.dataplanner.ModifyDataPathBackendRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Activate the data path and/or configure automatic data path activation. Setting the auto activation timeout to null disables auto activation. The unavailable media policy determines the policy for how to handle "temporarily unavailable" media when creating GET and VERIFY jobs. For example, consider 2 cases: (1) a blob is on tapes A and B; (2) a blob is on tape B. Consider that tape B is temporarily unavailable (e.g. its tape partition is quiesced, or its library is powered down). With policy ALLOW, in case 1, we could service the job from either tape A or B, and in case 2, we would service the job from tape B. With policy DISCOURAGED, in case 1, we would service the job from tape A, and in case 2, we would service the job from tape B. With policy DISALLOW, in case 1, we would service the job from tape A, and in case 2, we would fail the job creation.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [activated, allowNewJobRequests, alwaysRollback, autoActivateTimeoutInMins, autoInspect, cacheAvailableRetryAfterInSeconds, defaultVerifyDataAfterImport, defaultVerifyDataPriorToImport, iomCacheLimitationPercent, iomEnabled, maxAggregatedBlobsPerChunk, maxNumberOfConcurrentJobs, partiallyVerifyLastPercentOfTapes, poolSafetyEnabled, unavailableMediaPolicy, unavailablePoolMaxJobRetryInMins, unavailableTapePartitionMaxJobRetryInMins, verifyCheckpointBeforeRead]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.group.VerifyUserIsMemberOfGroupRequestHandler

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.AllocateJobChunkRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Allocate a specific job chunk that is part of a PUT job before PUT'ing the objects to avoid 307 retries on the object PUTs, which adversely impact DS3 server performance and require you to perpetually provide the object data stream on PUTs for every retry. The ID that must be supplied for this request is the job chunk's ID. Use of this request handler is discouraged. See GetJobChunksReadyForClientProcessingRequestHandler for the preferred way to get chunks to work on, which works for both GETs and PUTs, and has Spectra managing your working chunk window, rather than moving that complexity to you.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.CancelActiveJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delegates to the CancelJobRequestHandler

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.CancelAllActiveJobsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delegates to the CancelAllJobsRequestHandler

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.CancelAllJobsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Forcibly cancels all jobs that are in progress, or, if filtering parameters are specified, cancels all jobs that are in progress that match the specified filtering criteria. Any objects in the job that have been partially written to their permanent data stores (regardless as to whether or not they have been completely written to cache) will be deleted and any space object pieces consume on permanent data stores marked as eligible for reclamation. For a less forced way to cancel jobs, look at TruncateAllJobsRequestHandler.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.CancelJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Forcibly cancel a job that is in progress. Any objects in the job that have been partially written to their permanent data stores (regardless as to whether or not they have been completely written to cache) will be deleted and any space object pieces consume on permanent data stores marked as eligible for reclamation. For a less forced way to cancel jobs, look at TruncateJobRequestHandler.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.ClearAllCanceledJobsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Clears all canceled job history.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.ClearAllCompletedJobsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Clears all completed job history.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.CloseAggregatingJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Closes an existing aggregating job so no more jobs may be appended to it.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.CreateGetJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Create a job to GET / read objects. An XML payload must be sent to describe the GET job to create. If not specified, the job priority and chunk client processing order guarantee will default to the default configured on the data policy for the bucket that the job is for.The NONE chunk client processing order guarantee will achieve maximum performance and does not require pre-allocation in the cache, but requires the client of the job to get chunks as they become available, even if they become available out of order. The job will time out and be canceled if there has been no activity for the job in at least 24 hours. Any of the following is considered job activity: (i) I/O transfer to the client, or (ii) modify job requests (even if the modify request doesn't actually modify anything). Folders are not allowed to be part of a GET job (since folders are always zero-length). Objects that are zero-length that are requested as part of a GET job will be ignored (and will not be included in the resulting GET job created).
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [operation], Optional: [aggregating, chunkClientProcessingOrderGuarantee, deadJobCleanupAllowed, implicitJobIdResolution, name, priority, protected]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.CreatePutJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Create a job to PUT / write objects. An XML payload must be sent to describe the PUT job to create. If not specified, the job priority and write optimization will default to the default configured on the data policy for the bucket that the job is for. In the response payload for creating a PUT job, the objects sent in the request will be broken up and organized into chunks. Chunks must be sent by the client in order; however, objects within a given chunk may be sent in any order. The job will time out and be canceled if there has been no activity for the job in at least 24 hours. If a job is canceled due to lack of activity, it will be non-forcibly canceled, which ensures that any objects transmitted in their entirety by the client will be retained (and only objects that are incomplete will be canceled). Any of the following is considered job activity: (i) I/O transfer from the client, or (ii) modify job requests (even if the modify request doesn't actually modify anything). Folders (which are always zero-length) and zero-length objects must be explicitly PUT after creating a PUT job, which gives the client an opportunity to upload metadata for the folder or zero-length object at that time. Note that, if the force flag is not specified, that VerifySafeToCreatePutJobRequestHandler will be invoked as a prerequisite step to creating the PUT job, to ensure it is safe to do so.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [operation], Optional: [aggregating, deadJobCleanupAllowed, force, ignore_naming_conflicts, implicitJobIdResolution, max_upload_size, minimizeSpanningAcrossMedia, name, pre_allocate_job_space, priority, protected, verifyAfterWrite]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.CreateVerifyJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Create a job to verify objects. An XML payload must be sent to describe the verify job to create. If not specified, the job priority will default to the default configured on the data policy for the bucket that the job is for. A verify job reads data from tape and verifies that the CRC of the data read matches the expected CRC. Verify jobs will ALWAYS read from tape - even if the data currently resides in cache.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [operation], Optional: [aggregating, name, priority]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.DeleteJobCreationFailureRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Deletes the job creation failure specified.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.GetJobChunkRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get the specified job chunk (with the node and object information for the chunk).

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.GetJobChunksReadyForClientProcessingRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get all job chunks for a given job that are ready for client processing. For PUT jobs, this will allocate a working window of job chunks if possible and return the job chunks that the client can upload. Any chunk returned is fully allocated, meaning that you do not have to handle 307 retries on subsequent PUTs for said chunks, which adversely impacts DS3 server performance and requires you to perpetually attach the object data stream for every retry. For GET jobs, this will respond with which job chunks have been loaded into cache and are ready for download.
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/job_chunk/?job={unique identifier or attribute}[&job_chunk={unique identifier or attribute}][&preferred_number_of_chunks={32-bit integer}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.GetJobCreationFailuresRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get the list of job creation failures.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.GetJobEntriesRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get all job entries for a given job.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [jobId], Optional: [last_page, page_length, page_offset, page_start_marker]
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/job_chunk_dao/?job_id={unique identifier or attribute}[&last_page][&page_length={32-bit integer}][&page_offset={32-bit integer}][&page_start_marker={unique identifier or attribute}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.GetJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get the specified job. The status field of the job returned will designate whether the job is IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, or CANCELED. Jobs that have completed or been canceled will be automatically cleared after 30 days. A job can be canceled explicitly by a user or automatically once internal timeouts are reached due to client inactivity.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.GetJobSummaryRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get a progress summary of the specified job.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.GetJobToReplicateRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Returns a payload that represents a job that a client desires to replicate to another Black Pearl. This payload is not intended to be consumed directly by the client, but rather, to be forwarded to another Black Pearl to replicate the job.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.GetJobsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get all in-progress jobs. If the full details request parameter is specified, also includes recent completed and canceled jobs.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.ModifyActiveJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delegates to the ModifyJobRequestHandler.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [createdAt, deadJobCleanupAllowed, name, priority, protected]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.ModifyJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Modify a job that is in progress.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [createdAt, deadJobCleanupAllowed, name, priority, protected]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.ReplicatePutJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Replicates a job from another Black Pearl to this Black Pearl, ensuring that the object ids, blob ids, object versions, and blobbing structures for each object are identical, so that the objects are recognized as identical across the Black Pearls that replication is performed across. Clients must feed the response payload returned by GetPutJobToReplicateRequestHandler on the source Black Pearl in as the request payload for theReplicatePutJobRequestHandler on the target Black Pearl.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.StageObjectsJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Create a job to stage objects into the cache. An XML payload must be sent to describe the GET job to create. If not specified, the job priority and chunk client processing order guarantee will default to the default configured on the data policy for the bucket that the job is for.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.TruncateActiveJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delegates to the TruncateJobRequestHandler.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.TruncateAllActiveJobsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delegates to the TruncateAllJobsRequestHandler.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.TruncateAllJobsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancels all jobs that are in progress, or, if filtering parameters are specified, cancels all jobs that are in progress that match the specified filtering criteria. Any objects in the job that have been written in their entirety to their permanent data stores will be retained. For a more forced way to cancel jobs, look at CancelAllJobsRequestHandler.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.TruncateJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancel a job that is in progress. Any objects in the job that have been written in their entirety to their permanent data stores will be retained. For a more forced way to cancel jobs, look at CancelJobRequestHandler.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.job.VerifySafeToCreatePutJobRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Checks to see if the specified user can safely create a PUT job for the specified bucket at this time. It is not safe to create a PUT job if at least one target is offline that we need to replicate to.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.node.GetNodeRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get the specified node.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.node.GetNodesRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get all nodes.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [last_page, page_length, page_offset, page_start_marker]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.node.ModifyNodeRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Modify the specified node. The human and DNS names of the node can be modified. If a DNS name isn't configured for a node, its data path IP address will be used when the TCP/IP address of the node must be given to a client. If a DNS name is configured for a node, this DNS name (or IP address) will be used, regardless as to whether or not the data path IP address changes after the DNS name is configured.

RequestHandler.Documentation: Recursively deletes the folder specified and all folders and objects beneath it.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.object.GetBlobPersistenceRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Gets existence and checksum information for the specified blob ids. This request handler is intended to be used by other DS3 appliances that are replicating to this DS3 appliance. This request handler is not intended to be used by end users directly.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.object.GetObjectRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get an S3 object.
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/object/{unique identifier or attribute}/?bucket_id={unique identifier or attribute}

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.object.GetObjectsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get all S3 objects.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [bucketId, end_date, last_page, latest, name, page_length, page_offset, page_start_marker, start_date, type]
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/object/[?end_date={64-bit integer}][&last_page][&page_length={32-bit integer}][&page_offset={32-bit integer}][&page_start_marker={unique identifier or attribute}][&start_date={64-bit integer}][&bucket_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&latest={true|false}][&name={text}][&type={data|folder}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.object.GetObjectsWithFullDetailsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get all S3 objects with detailed information in the response payload.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [full_details], Optional: [bucketId, end_date, include_physical_placement, last_page, latest, name, page_length, page_offset, page_start_marker, start_date, type]
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/object/?full_details[&end_date={64-bit integer}][&include_physical_placement][&last_page][&page_length={32-bit integer}][&page_offset={32-bit integer}][&page_start_marker={unique identifier or attribute}][&start_date={64-bit integer}][&bucket_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&latest={true|false}][&name={text}][&type={data|folder}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.object.GetPhysicalPlacementForObjectsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Gets the physical placement of the objects specified. Objects are specified the same they are for creating a GET job. The lack of existence of an object or the lack of its having physical placement will not result in any exception. If the full_details query param is specified, physical placement will be shown on a per-object-piece basis. If the full_details query param is not specified, a summary physical placement will be provided without per-object-piece breakups.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.object.GetPhysicalPlacementForObjectsWithFullDetailsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Gets the physical placement of the objects specified. Objects are specified the same they are for creating a GET job. The lack of existence of an object or the lack of its having physical placement will not result in any exception. If the full_details query param is specified, physical placement will be shown on a per-object-piece basis. If the full_details query param is not specified, a summary physical placement will be provided without per-object-piece breakups.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.object.UndeleteObjectRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Undeletes an object on a versioned system if available. Can only be used on the most recent version of that object.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.object.VerifyPhysicalPlacementForObjectsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Gets the physical placement of the objects specified. Objects are specified the same they are for creating a GET job. A 404 not found error will be returned if one or more objects either (i) does not exist, or (ii) has no physical placement. If the full_details query param is specified, physical placement will be shown on a per-object-piece basis. If the full_details query param is not specified, a summary physical placement will be provided without per-object-piece breakups.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.object.VerifyPhysicalPlacementForObjectsWithFullDetailsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Gets the physical placement of the objects specified. Objects are specified the same they are for creating a GET job. A 404 not found error will be returned if one or more objects either (i) does not exist, or (ii) has no physical placement. If the full_details query param is specified, physical placement will be shown on a per-object-piece basis. If the full_details query param is not specified, a summary physical placement will be provided without per-object-piece breakups.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.CancelImportOnAllPoolsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancel all pending pool imports. Imports that have already begun cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.CancelImportPoolRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancel a pending pool import on the specified pool. If the import has already begun, it cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.CancelVerifyOnAllPoolsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancel all pending pool verifications. Verifies that have already begun cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.CancelVerifyPoolRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancel a pending pool verification on the specified pool. If the verify has already begun, it cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.CompactAllPoolsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Pool compaction is done automatically, periodically in the background. Forces a compaction to be scheduled immediately for all eligible pools, without having to wait for the automatic, periodic scheduling.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.CompactPoolRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Pool compaction is done automatically, periodically in the background. When a pool is compacted, it is first checked to see if it can be reclaimed in its entirety and unassigned from its assigned storage domain, making it available to be allocated to any storage domain. A pool can only be reclaimed in its entirety if it contains no data on it. If the pool cannot be reclaimed in its entirety, its usage level will be checked and data that is eligible for deletion will be deleted as necessary to compact the pool down to where it has plenty of space for new data. Forces a compaction to be scheduled immediately, without having to wait for the automatic, periodic scheduling of compaction.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.CreatePoolPartitionRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Create a pool partition. A pool partition may contain any number of pools and may be added as a member of a storage domain.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.DeallocatePoolRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Deallocates the specified pool, which contains no data.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.DeletePermanentlyLostPoolRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Deletes the specified pool which has been permanently lost, recording any data loss that has occurred as a result.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.DeletePoolFailureRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Deletes the specified pool failure.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.DeletePoolPartitionRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delete the specified pool partition.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.ForcePoolEnvironmentRefreshRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Forces the pool environment to be refreshed at the earliest convenience. Note that the pool environment is updated automatically based on pool environment change events. Invoking this method should never be necessary unless an event is missed and thus we fail to update the pool environment.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.FormatAllForeignPoolsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Formats all foreign pools, permanently deleting all data on them and taking ownership of them for this appliance.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.FormatForeignPoolRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Formats the specified foreign pool, permanently deleting all data on it and taking ownership of it for this appliance.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.GetBlobsOnPoolRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get the object pieces on the specified pool.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.GetPoolFailuresRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get all pool failures.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [errorMessage, last_page, page_length, page_offset, page_start_marker, poolId, type]
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/pool_failure/[?last_page][&page_length={32-bit integer}][&page_offset={32-bit integer}][&page_start_marker={unique identifier or attribute}][&error_message={text}][&pool_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&type={blob_read_failed|data_checkpoint_failure|data_checkpoint_missing|format_failed|import_failed|import_incomplete|import_failed_due_to_take_ownership_failure|import_failed_due_to_data_integrity|inspect_failed|quiesced|read_failed|verify_failed|write_failed}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.GetPoolPartitionRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get the specified pool partition.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.GetPoolPartitionsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get all pool partitions.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [last_page, name, page_length, page_offset, page_start_marker, type]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.GetPoolRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get the specified pool.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.GetPoolsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get all pools.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [assignedToStorageDomain, bucketId, guid, health, lastVerified, last_page, name, page_length, page_offset, page_start_marker, partitionId, poweredOn, state, storageDomainMemberId, type]
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/pool/[?last_page][&page_length={32-bit integer}][&page_offset={32-bit integer}][&page_start_marker={unique identifier or attribute}][&assigned_to_storage_domain={true|false}][&bucket_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&guid={text}][&health={ok|degraded}][&last_verified={date}][&name={text}][&partition_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&powered_on={true|false}][&state={blank|normal|lost|foreign|import_pending|import_in_progress}][&storage_domain_member_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&type={nearline|online}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.ImportAllPoolsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Imports all foreign Black Pearl pools. Note that this will not import a pool that has arbitrary data not from a Black Pearl on it. If one of more buckets being imported does not exist yet, the user ID and data policy to use for any buckets that have to be created must be specified. The storage domain to ingress the pool into may optionally be specified. If not specified, the system will attempt to automatically determine the most sane storage domain to place the pool into. It is highly recommended to verify data prior to import whenever it's possible that what's being imported may contain objects with the same name in the same bucket as an already existing object that's different. Without verifying data prior to import, it is possible for the old object to be replaced with the one being imported, even if the one being imported is partially corrupt and cannot be read.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [operation], Optional: [dataPolicyId, priority, storageDomainId, userId, verifyDataAfterImport, verifyDataPriorToImport]
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/pool/?operation=import[&data_policy_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&priority={critical|urgent|high|normal|low|background}][&storage_domain_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&user_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&verify_data_after_import={critical|urgent|high|normal|low|background}][&verify_data_prior_to_import={true|false}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.ImportPoolRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Imports the specified foreign Black Pearl pool. Note that this will not import a pool that has arbitrary data not from a Black Pearl on it. If one of more buckets being imported does not exist yet, the user ID and data policy to use for any buckets that have to be created must be specified. The storage domain to ingress the pool into may optionally be specified. If not specified, the system will attempt to automatically determine the most sane storage domain to place the pool into. It is highly recommended to verify data prior to import whenever it's possible that what's being imported may contain objects with the same name in the same bucket as an already existing object that's different. Without verifying data prior to import, it is possible for the old object to be replaced with the one being imported, even if the one being imported is partially corrupt and cannot be read.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [operation], Optional: [dataPolicyId, priority, storageDomainId, userId, verifyDataAfterImport, verifyDataPriorToImport]
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/pool/{unique identifier or attribute}/?operation=import[&data_policy_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&priority={critical|urgent|high|normal|low|background}][&storage_domain_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&user_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&verify_data_after_import={critical|urgent|high|normal|low|background}][&verify_data_prior_to_import={true|false}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.ModifyAllPoolsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Modifies the quiesced state across all pools to the value specified.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.ModifyPoolPartitionRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Modify the specified pool partition.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.ModifyPoolRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Modify the specified pool.
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/pool/{unique identifier or attribute}/[?partition_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&quiesced={no|pending|yes}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.VerifyAllPoolsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Pool verification is done automatically, periodically in the background. Forces a verification to be scheduled immediately for all eligible pools, without having to wait for the automatic, periodic scheduling.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.pool.VerifyPoolRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Pool verification is done automatically, periodically in the background. When a pool is verified, blobs that should be persisted on the pool are checked to ensure they do exist and are valid. If a blob is missing or is otherwise invalid, a blob loss failure shall be generated.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.storagedomain.ConvertStorageDomainToDs3TargetRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Converts the specified storage domain to the specified DS3 target. Once converted, there is no conversion back. In converting the storage domain over, all blobs persisted locally will be migrated to be persisted at the target.
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/storage_domain/{unique identifier or attribute}/?convert_to_ds3_target={unique identifier or attribute}

RequestHandler.Documentation: Create a storage domain.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [name], Optional: [autoEjectMediaFullThreshold, autoEjectUponCron, autoEjectUponJobCancellation, autoEjectUponJobCompletion, autoEjectUponMediaFull, ltfsFileNaming, maxTapeFragmentationPercent, maximumAutoVerificationFrequencyInDays, mediaEjectionAllowed, secureMediaAllocation, verifyPriorToAutoEject, writeOptimization]

RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [storageDomainId, tapePartitionId, tapeType], Optional: [autoCompactionThreshold, writePreference]

RequestHandler.Documentation: Deletes the specified storage domain member.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.storagedomain.DeleteStorageDomainRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Deletes the specified storage domain.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.storagedomain.GetStorageDomainFailuresRequestHandler

RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/storage_domain_member/[?last_page][&page_length={32-bit integer}][&page_offset={32-bit integer}][&page_start_marker={unique identifier or attribute}][&pool_partition_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&state={normal|exclusion_in_progress}][&storage_domain_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&tape_partition_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&tape_type={lto5|lto6|lto7|ltom8|lto8|lto9|lto10|lto10p|lto_cleaning_tape|ts_jc|ts_jy|ts_jk|ts_jd|ts_jz|ts_jl|ts_jm|ts_je|ts_jv|ts_jf|ts_jn|ts_cleaning_tape|unknown|forbidden}][&write_preference={high|normal|low|never_select}]

RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [autoEjectMediaFullThreshold, autoEjectUponCron, autoEjectUponJobCancellation, autoEjectUponJobCompletion, autoEjectUponMediaFull, ltfsFileNaming, maxTapeFragmentationPercent, maximumAutoVerificationFrequencyInDays, mediaEjectionAllowed, name, secureMediaAllocation, verifyPriorToAutoEject, writeOptimization]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.system.ForceFeatureKeyValidationRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Forces a synchronous feature key validation. Feature keys are validated regularly automatically, and invoking this request handler shouldn't ever be necessary to an end user, as validation will never re-enable a disabled key (it can only disable an enabled key).

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.system.GetAbmConfigRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Gets info about the current ABM (Advanced Bucket Management) configuration.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.system.GetFeatureKeysRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Gets all feature keys.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [errorMessage, expirationDate, key, last_page, page_length, page_offset, page_start_marker]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.system.GetRequestHandlersFormalApiContractRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Gets a formal contract for the API that can be easily parsed.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.system.GetRequestHandlersRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get information (and self-documentation) on all the request handlers supported by your DS3 server. Note that the version for a request handler is always of the form X.Y, where X is the major revision and Yis the minor revision. The major revision is always an integer. The minor revision is always an MD5 checksum.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.system.GetSystemFailuresRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Gets the current system failures that must be fixed.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [], Optional: [errorMessage, last_page, page_length, page_offset, page_start_marker, type]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.system.GetSystemInformationRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Get basic system information, including version and build information, and the system serial number.Note that the version is always of the form X.Y, where X is the MD5 checksum across all request handler majorrevisions and Y is the MD5 checksum across all request handler full versions (including the minor revision).

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.system.ResetInstanceIdentifierRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Resets and regenerates the instance identifier. Resetting the instance identifier is only necessary in rare circumstances where there are two branches of an instance running at the same time. Of of the two branches must have its instance identifier reset if the two instances are to replicate between each other. The most common occurrence of this is when one instance is initially configured with dual copy where one copy goes on one library and the other copy goes on the other, then a new Black Pearl is added and one library and the new BP moved to another physical location. In this case, both BPs would start with the same database and each would have the persistence rule and storage domain for the remote library converted to a replication rule. This requires that one or both sites have their instance identifier reset first.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.system.VerifySystemHealthRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Verifies that the system appears to be online and functioning normally. If critical components in thedata path front end are unresponsive, an error will be generated.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CancelEjectOnAllTapesRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancels all pending tape ejects. Ejects that have already begun cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CancelEjectTapeRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancels a pending tape eject on the specified tape. If the eject has already begun, it cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CancelFormatOnAllTapesRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancel all pending tape formats. Formats that have already begun cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CancelFormatTapeRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancel a pending tape format on the specified tape. If the format has already begun, it cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CancelImportOnAllTapesRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancel all pending tape imports. Imports that have already begun cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CancelImportTapeRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancel a pending tape import on the specified tape. If the import has already begun, it cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CancelOnlineOnAllTapesRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancels all pending tape onlining. Onlining that has already begun cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CancelOnlineTapeRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancels a pending tape onlining on the specified tape. If the eject has already begun to be brought online, it cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CancelTestTapeDriveRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancels any pending test on the specified tape drive.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CancelVerifyOnAllTapesRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancels all pending tape verifications. Verifications that have already begun cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CancelVerifyTapeRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cancels a pending tape verification on the specified tape. If the verify has already begun, it cannot be cancelled.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CleanTapeDriveRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Cleans the specified tape drive.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CreateDriveDumpRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Creates a drive dump of the specified tape drive.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.CreateTapeDensityDirectiveRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Create a directive for formatting tapes of a particular type at the specified density for the specified partition. The density specified is the tape drive type that the tape should be compatible with. The tape will be formatted at the highest density supported by the specified tape drive.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [density, partitionId, tapeType], Optional: []

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.DeletePermanentlyLostTapeRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Deletes the specified tape which has been permanently lost, recording any data loss that has occurred as a result.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.DeleteTapeDensityDirectiveRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delete the specified tape density directive.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.DeleteTapeDriveRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delete the specified offline tape drive.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.DeleteTapeFailureRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Deletes the specified tape failure.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.DeleteTapePartitionFailureRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Deletes the specified tape partition failure.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.DeleteTapePartitionRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Delete the specified offline tape partition. Any tapes that had data on them in that partition will be de-associated from the partition. Any tapes without data on them and tape drives that were associated with the partition will be deleted.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.EjectAllTapesRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Ejects all tapes that are eligible to be ejected. If a tape is in use, it will be ejected once it is no longer in use.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.EjectStorageDomainBlobsRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Ejects all tapes that are eligible to be ejected within the storage domain specified for the blob payload included. If a tape is in use, it will be ejected once it is no longer in use.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [blobs, bucketId, operation, storage_domain], Optional: [ejectLabel, ejectLocation]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.EjectStorageDomainRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Ejects all tapes that are eligible to be ejected within the storage domain specified. If a tape is in use, it will be ejected once it is no longer in use.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [operation, storage_domain], Optional: [bucketId, ejectLabel, ejectLocation]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.EjectTapeRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Ejects the tape specified. If the tape is in use, it will be ejected once it is no longer in use.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.ForceTapeEnvironmentRefreshRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Forces the tape environment to be refreshed at the earliest convenience. Note that the tape environment is updated automatically based on tape environment change events. Invoking this method should never be necessary unless an event is missed and thus we fail to update the tape environment.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.FormatAllTapesRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Format all tapes that have not already been formatted and are not already in use by the system. Tapes that contain data written externally of this product will be formatted even without the force flag. Tapes that contain data written by this product (but not this specific appliance) will not be formatted unless the force flag is specified. Tapes that contain data written by this appliance cannot be deleted in this manner. You must delete the objects and/or buckets before you can format the tape they reside on.

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.FormatTapeRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Format the specified tape if it has not already been formatted and is not already in use by the system. Tapes that contain data written externally of this product will be formatted even without the force flag. Tapes that contain data written by this product (but not this specific appliance) will not be formatted unless the force flag is specified. Tapes that contain data written by this appliance cannot be deleted in this manner. You must delete the objects and/or buckets before you can format the tape they reside on.

RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/tape/[?bucket={text}][&last_page][&page_length={32-bit integer}][&page_offset={32-bit integer}][&page_start_marker={unique identifier or attribute}][&partition={text}][&sort_by={text}][&storage_domain={text}][&assigned_to_storage_domain={true|false}][&available_raw_capacity={64-bit integer}][&bar_code={text}][&bucket_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&eject_label={text}][&eject_location={text}][&full_of_data={true|false}][&last_verified={date}][&partially_verified_end_of_tape={date}][&partition_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&previous_state={normal|offline|online_pending|online_in_progress|pending_inspection|unknown|data_checkpoint_failure|data_checkpoint_failure_due_to_read_only|data_checkpoint_missing|ltfs_with_foreign_data|raw_import_pending|raw_import_in_progress|foreign|import_pending|import_in_progress|incompatible|lost|bad|cannot_format_due_to_write_protection|serial_number_mismatch|bar_code_missing|auto_compaction_in_progress|format_pending|format_in_progress|eject_to_ee_in_progress|eject_from_ee_pending|ejected}][&serial_number={text}][&state={normal|offline|online_pending|online_in_progress|pending_inspection|unknown|data_checkpoint_failure|data_checkpoint_failure_due_to_read_only|data_checkpoint_missing|ltfs_with_foreign_data|raw_import_pending|raw_import_in_progress|foreign|import_pending|import_in_progress|incompatible|lost|bad|cannot_format_due_to_write_protection|serial_number_mismatch|bar_code_missing|auto_compaction_in_progress|format_pending|format_in_progress|eject_to_ee_in_progress|eject_from_ee_pending|ejected}][&storage_domain_member_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&type={lto5|lto6|lto7|ltom8|lto8|lto9|lto10|lto10p|lto_cleaning_tape|ts_jc|ts_jy|ts_jk|ts_jd|ts_jz|ts_jl|ts_jm|ts_je|ts_jv|ts_jf|ts_jn|ts_cleaning_tape|unknown|forbidden}][&verify_pending={critical|urgent|high|normal|low|background}][&write_protected={true|false}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.ImportAllTapesRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Imports all foreign Black Pearl tapes. Note that this will not import tapes that have arbitrary data not from a Black Pearl on them. If one of more buckets being imported does not exist yet, the user ID and data policy to use for any buckets that have to be created must be specified. The storage domain to ingress the tape into may optionally be specified. If not specified, the system will attempt to automatically determine the most sane storage domain to place the tape into. It is highly recommended to verify data prior to import whenever it's possible that what's being imported may contain objects with the same name in the same bucket as an already existing object that's different. Without verifying data prior to import, it is possible for the old object to be replaced with the one being imported, even if the one being imported is partially corrupt and cannot be read.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [operation], Optional: [dataPolicyId, priority, storageDomainId, userId, verifyDataAfterImport, verifyDataPriorToImport]
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/tape/?operation=import[&data_policy_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&priority={critical|urgent|high|normal|low|background}][&storage_domain_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&user_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&verify_data_after_import={critical|urgent|high|normal|low|background}][&verify_data_prior_to_import={true|false}]

RequestHandler.Name: com.spectralogic.s3.server.handler.reqhandler.spectrads3.tape.ImportTapeRequestHandler
RequestHandler.Documentation: Imports the specified foreign Black Pearl tape. Note that this will not import a tape that has arbitrary data not from a Black Pearl on it. If one of more buckets being imported does not exist yet, the user ID and data policy to use for any buckets that have to be created must be specified. The storage domain to ingress the tape into may optionally be specified. If not specified, the system will attempt to automatically determine the most sane storage domain to place the tape into. It is highly recommended to verify data prior to import whenever it's possible that what's being imported may contain objects with the same name in the same bucket as an already existing object that's different. Without verifying data prior to import, it is possible for the old object to be replaced with the one being imported, even if the one being imported is partially corrupt and cannot be read.
RequestHandler.RequestRequirements: Query Parameters Required: [operation], Optional: [dataPolicyId, priority, storageDomainId, userId, verifyDataAfterImport, verifyDataPriorToImport]
RequestHandler.SampleUrl: http[s]://datapathdnsnameofappliance/_rest_/tape/{unique identifier or attribute}/?operation=import[&data_policy_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&priority={critical|urgent|high|normal|low|background}][&storage_domain_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&user_id={unique identifier or attribute}][&verify_data_after_import={critical|urgent|high|normal|low|background}][&verify_data_prior_to_import={true|false}]
